key_insight: On NbP(001), hydrogen adsorption is only weakly affected by spin-orbit coupling, but topological surface states strongly shape Fermi-level hybridization and induce a finite spin polarization on the adsorbed H.
engineering_relevance: This suggests topological semimetal surfaces can be tuned for electrocatalytic interfaces where adsorption thermodynamics stay stable while interfacial spin and charge responses are engineered.
method: DFT calculations comparing SOC off vs SOC on surface electronic structure
Wannier-based surface spectral functions plus orbital projections
Projected crystal orbital Hamilton population analysis for bonding and hybridization
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a direct theory-based comparison of two electronic regimes and finds only small changes in hydrogen adsorption free energy, while identifying state-resolved bonding and H-projected current-induced spin polarization.
watch_item: The conclusions are based on computed surface models for a specific NbP(001) termination, so real catalytic performance may depend on surface reconstruction, defects, coverage, and experimental conditions.
paper_title: Hydrogen Chemisorption and Current-Induced Spin Polarization on NbP
